message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Activate test: quote-only scenario via stdin produces {results:[{premium:<int>}]} on stdout (line 125).

This is a CLI test. The CLI entry point is at src/cli.ts. Test should invoke the CLI as a subprocess (spawn node + tsx) feeding stdin JSON and reading stdout JSON, or test by importing a `runCli` function that accepts (input string) and returns (output string + exit code).

Simplest approach: export from src/cli.ts a `runScenario(scenarioJson: string): {stdout: string, stderr: string, exitCode: number}` function and have a thin shebang wrapper consume stdin/stdout. Test the function directly.

The scenario JSON shape (from prompt.md):
{ "customer": {"yearsWithMHPCO": N}, "steps": [{"op": "quote", "items": [...]}, ...] }
Output: { "results": [{"premium": N}, {"payout": N, "remainingCap": N}, ...] }

For this first test: just a single quote step.

Current state: 30 tests passing
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts (and src/cli.ts needs to be created)

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Green after completion.
You MUST output the full Step 7 block verbatim.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

eslint.config.mjs
example-mapping
metrics.json
node_modules
package.json
pnpm-lock.yaml
prompt.md
run.log
src

1	# MHPCO Claim Office — Example Mapping
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	The MHPCO system supports two operations within a scenario for a single customer:
6	
7	- **quote**: compute premium for a list of items
8	- **claim**: process damage against a previously-created policy (referenced by 0-based step index)
9	
10	The CLI lives at `src/cli.ts`, reads a JSON scenario from stdin, writes a JSON
11	result document to stdout. Errors exit non-zero and write to stderr.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## Rules
16	
17	### R1. Base premiums and insurance values
18	
19	Per main item:
20	- Sword: 1000 G insurance value, 100 G base premium
21	- Amulet: 600 G / 60 G
22	- Staff:  800 G / 80 G
23	- Potion: 400 G / 40 G
24	
25	Components (rune, moonstone):
26	- 250 G insurance value, 25 G base premium each
27	- A "block" of exactly 3 alike components → 60 G base premium (replaces 3×25 = 75)
28	
29	### R2. Components — "alike" means same type
30	
31	Disambiguation from ❓: "alike" = same component type. A rune and a moonstone
32	are NOT alike, even though both are components.
33	
34	### R3. Premium modifiers
35	
36	Item-specific (applied to the affected item's base premium):
37	- Cursed: +50%
38	- High enchantment (level ≥ 5): +30%
39	
40	Policy-wide (applied to the policy base premium = sum of all item base premiums after item modifiers):
41	- Loyalty (≥ 2 years with MHPCO): −20%
42	- First insurance: +10% (per-item — see R3a)
43	- Follow-up contract (any quote after the customer's first in this scenario): −15%
44	
45	Final:
46	- Add 5 G processing fee.
47	
48	### R3a. First-insurance surcharge is per-item
49	
50	Disambiguation from ❓: "first insurance" means each item is treated as a first
51	insurance regardless of customer history; the surcharge always applies to every
52	item in any quote. (See "Long-standing customer's second contract" example —
53	+10G first insurance is still added on the second contract.)
54	
55	### R3b. Modifier ordering (derived from integration examples)
56	
57	For a given item:
58	- item premium = base × (1 + 0.5 if cursed) × (1 + 0.3 if enchant ≥ 5)
59	  - Equivalently, the spec presents the two surcharges as additive on the base
60	    (cursed +50G, enchant +30G on a 100G sword sums to 180G). Both
61	    interpretations give 180 in the spec's examples — use additive on base for
62	    item-specific modifiers to match the spec's worked sums exactly:
63	    - cursed item premium = base + base×0.5
64	    - high-ench item premium = base + base×0.3
65	    - both = base + base×0.5 + base×0.3 (e.g. cursed sword ench 7: 100 + 50 + 30 = 180)
66	- per-item first-insurance surcharge = base × 0.10 (added to that item)
67	
68	Policy base premium = sum of (per-item base + per-item surcharges + per-item first-insurance)
69	
70	Then policy-wide modifiers add/subtract from the policy base:
71	- loyalty discount = policy base × 0.20 (subtract)
72	- follow-up discount = policy base × 0.15 (subtract)
73	
74	Then +5 G fee at the very end.
75	
76	Verification against "Long-standing customer's second contract":
77	- cursed sword (ench 7): base 100 + 50 (curse) + 30 (high-ench) + 10 (first-ins) = 190
78	- −20G loyalty (20% of 100) − 15G follow-up (15% of 100) = 190 − 20 − 15 = 155
79	- +5G fee = 160. ✓
80	
81	Verification against "Newcomer with cursed sword":
82	- cursed sword (ench 3): 100 + 50 (curse) + 10 (first-ins) = 160
83	- no loyalty, no follow-up
84	- +5G fee = 165. ✓
85	
86	Verification against "cursed sword + plain amulet" (no other modifiers):
87	- cursed sword: 100 + 50 = 150; plain amulet: 60
88	- policy base before first-insurance: 210
89	- (the spec wording: "policy base premium 160 G; the cursed surcharge adds
90	  50 G… → 210 G before further modifiers and fee")
91	- Note: the spec's "210 G before further modifiers and fee" excludes
92	  first-insurance, which is a per-item surcharge that still applies later.
93	
94	### R4. Rounding in MHPCO's favor
95	
96	- Premiums rounded UP to whole G (ceiling)
97	- Payouts rounded DOWN to whole G (floor)
98	- Only the FINAL premium/payout is rounded; intermediate values stay fractional.
99	
100	### R5. Claim — deductible & cap
101	
102	- 100 G deductible per damage event (per entry in the `damages` array)
103	- Cap = 2 × insurance sum (sum of unmodified insurance values of all items in the policy)
104	- Premium modifiers do NOT raise the cap
105	- Block discount does NOT lower the cap (insurance sum uses raw 250 G per component)
106	
107	### R6. Claim — special reimbursement clauses
108	
109	- Item with enchantment ≥ 8 → 50% reimbursement of damage amount (before deductible)
110	- Item made of "dragon" material → 100% reimbursement (full reimbursement)
111	- If both clauses apply, the 50% rule wins (see "dragon, ench 9 → 400 G")
112	- Then the deductible is subtracted: payout = max(0, reimbursable − 100)
113	- Components (rune, moonstone) have no enchantment / material; no special clause applies
114	
115	### R7. Claim — multi-claim cap exhaustion
116	
117	- Cap accumulates across multiple claims on the same policy
118	- Each claim's payout is clipped to the remaining cap
119	- `remainingCap` in result = cap remaining AFTER this claim
120	
121	### R8. Claim — multi-item & damage matching
122	
123	- A policy may contain multiple items of the same type (e.g. two swords)
124	- Each entry in `damages` is a separate damage event with its own deductible
125	- If `damages` references more items of a type than the policy contains → error (non-zero exit, stderr)
126	- If `damages` references a type not in the policy at all → error
127	
128	### R9. CLI interface
129	
130	- Reads single JSON document from stdin (input schema in prompt.md)
131	- Writes single JSON document to stdout with `results` array (same order as `steps`)
132	- Quote result: `{premium: integer}`
133	- Claim result: `{payout: integer, remainingCap: integer}`
134	- Error conditions → non-zero exit, error to stderr, no `results` to stdout:
135	  - unknown item type in quote (e.g. `broomstick`)
136	  - claim references item type not in policy
137	  - claim has negative damage amount
138	  - claim has more damages of a type than the policy contains
139	
140	### R10. Edge cases
141	
142	- Empty item list → premium = 5 (just the fee)
143	- Customer with 0 years, no prior quote → no loyalty, no follow-up; first
144	  quote still triggers per-item first-insurance surcharge
145	- Each subsequent quote in the scenario is a "follow-up contract" → −15%
146	
147	---
148	
149	## Interface contract (binding field names)
150	
151	**Input (stdin):**
152	
153	```json
154	{
155	  "customer": {"yearsWithMHPCO": 5},
156	  "steps": [
157	    {
158	      "op": "quote",
159	      "items": [
160	        {"type": "amulet", "material": "silver", "enchantment": 2, "cursed": false}
161	      ]
162	    },
163	    {
164	      "op": "claim",
165	      "policy": 0,
166	      "incident": {
167	        "cause": "fire",
168	        "damages": [
169	          {"itemType": "amulet", "amount": 200}
170	        ]
171	      }
172	    }
173	  ]
174	}
175	```
176	
177	**Output (stdout):**
178	
179	```json
180	{"results": [{"premium": 71}, {"payout": 100, "remainingCap": 1100}]}
181	```
182	
183	(Values illustrative; the schema is binding.)
184	
185	Item types: `sword | amulet | staff | potion | rune | moonstone`
186	
187	---
188	
189	## Examples (with computed expected values)
190	
191	### E1. Base premiums (each item alone, no modifiers, year=0, first contract)
192	
193	- 1 sword (steel, ench 0) → 100 base + 10 first-ins + 5 fee = 115 G
194	- 1 amulet (silver, ench 0) → 60 + 6 + 5 = 71 G
195	- 1 staff (wood, ench 0) → 80 + 8 + 5 = 93 G
196	- 1 potion (glass, ench 0) → 40 + 4 + 5 = 49 G
197	
198	### E2. Empty item list
199	
200	- `items: []` → premium = 5 G (fee only)
201	
202	### E3. Components and blocks
203	
204	(year=0, no modifiers; component first-ins surcharge applies)
205	
206	- 2 runes → base 50 + 5 (first-ins) + 5 (fee) = 60 G
207	- 3 runes → base 60 (block) + 6 (10% of 60 first-ins) + 5 = 71 G
208	- 4 runes → base 100 + 10 + 5 = 115 G
209	- 7 runes → base 175 + 17.5 → 192.5 → 198 G (rounded up: 192.5 + 5 = 197.5, ceil → 198)
210	  - Actually: 175 + 17.5 (first-ins) = 192.5; +5 fee = 197.5; ceil = 198 G
211	  - This matches the rounding example (197.5 → 198)
212	
213	### E4. "Alike" = same type
214	
215	- 2 runes + 1 moonstone → 50 + 25 = 75 base; first-ins = 7.5; +5 fee = 87.5 → 88 G
216	- 3 runes + 3 moonstones → 60 + 60 = 120 base; first-ins = 12; +5 fee = 137 G
217	
218	### E5. Cursed surcharge is item-specific
219	
220	- Policy: cursed sword + plain amulet (year=0, first contract)
221	  - Item bases: sword 100, amulet 60
222	  - Curse on sword: +50
223	  - First-ins: sword 10, amulet 6
224	  - Policy base = 100 + 50 + 60 + 10 + 6 = 226
225	  - +5 fee = 231 G
226	  - (Note: the spec's "210 G before further modifiers and fee" excludes
227	    first-insurance; with first-ins added per-item we get 226, +5 = 231.)
228	
229	### E6. Enchantment threshold
230	
231	- Sword with exactly enchantment 5 (year=0, first contract, not cursed):
232	  - 100 + 30 (high-ench) + 13 (10% of 130 first-ins?) … wait — first-ins is 10% of base
233	  - Use: first-ins = 10% of base per item = 10
234	  - Item total = 100 + 30 + 10 = 140; +5 = 145 G
235	- Sword with enchantment 4 → no high-ench:
236	  - 100 + 10 + 5 = 115 G
237	- Sword with enchantment 5 and cursed:
238	  - 100 + 50 + 30 + 10 + 5 = 195 G
239	
240	### E7. Loyalty (≥ 2 years)
241	
242	- Customer 2 years, single plain sword (first contract, not cursed):
243	  - Item: 100 + 10 = 110
244	  - Loyalty: −20 (20% of 100 policy base premium of items pre-first-ins?
245	    or 20% of 110?)
246	  - Per R3b: loyalty applies to the policy base premium (sum of items including first-ins)
247	  - Actually, re-reading: "policy-wide modifiers… apply to the policy base
248	    premium (the sum of all item base premiums)"
249	  - "Sum of all item base premiums" = sum of unmodified bases = 100
250	  - Verification with the long-standing-customer example:
251	    - 100 + 50 + 30 + 10 = 190
252	    - loyalty −20 (20% of 100), follow-up −15 (15% of 100) = 190 − 35 = 155
253	    - +5 = 160 ✓
254	  - So **loyalty% and follow-up% are computed on the SUM OF UNMODIFIED BASE PREMIUMS**.
255	  - Customer 2 years, plain sword, first contract:
256	    - 100 (base) + 10 (first-ins) − 20 (loyalty 20% of 100) + 5 = 95 G
257	
258	### E8. Follow-up contract
259	
260	- The integration example "Long-standing customer's second contract" verifies:
261	  - 3 years, second quote in scenario, cursed sword ench 7:
262	  - 100 + 50 + 30 + 10 − 20 − 15 + 5 = 160 G ✓
263	
264	### E9. Rounding premiums up
265	
266	- A premium of 197.5 → 198 G (e.g. 7 runes scenario above)
267	
268	### E10. Newcomer with cursed sword (integration)
269	
270	- Customer 0 years, no prior contract, cursed sword steel ench 3
271	- 100 + 50 + 10 + 5 = 165 G ✓
272	
273	### E11. Claim — deductible per damage event
274	
275	- Two damages in one incident: sword damaged 500 G, amulet damaged 300 G (steel,
276	  low enchantment, no special clause)
277	- Payout = (500 − 100) + (300 − 100) = 400 + 200 = 600 G
278	
279	### E12. Claim — standard reimbursement minus deductible
280	
281	- Steel sword, ench 3, damage 500 → payout 400 G
282	- Rune (no enchantment/material), damage 200 → payout 100 G
283	
284	### E13. Claim — enchantment ≥ 8 (50%)
285	
286	- Steel sword, ench 9, damage 1000 → 500 (50%) − 100 (deductible) = 400 G
287	
288	### E14. Claim — dragon material (full)
289	
290	- Dragon sword, ench 5, damage 800 → 800 − 100 = 700 G
291	
292	### E15. Claim — both clauses → 50% wins
293	
294	- Dragon sword, ench 9, damage 1000 → 500 − 100 = 400 G
295	- Dragon sword, ench 8, damage 1000 → 500 − 100 = 400 G
296	
297	### E16. Claim — cap exhaustion across multiple claims
298	
299	- 1 sword insured: insurance sum 1000, cap 2000
300	- Claim 1 damage 1500 → payout = min(1500-100, 2000) = 1400; remainingCap = 600
301	- Claim 2 damage 1500 → desired = 1400, clipped to remaining cap 600 → payout 600; remainingCap = 0
302	
303	### E17. Cap not affected by premium modifiers / block discount
304	
305	- Cursed sword: cap = 2000 (not affected by premium 165)
306	- Sword + 3 runes: insurance sum = 1000 + 750 = 1750, cap = 3500
307	
308	### E18. Multiple swords in one policy
309	
310	- Two swords: insurance sum 2000, cap 4000
311	- Dragon attack damages both swords; both damages have own deductible
312	- (Steel swords, ench 3, each damaged 500): payout = 400 + 400 = 800
313	
314	### E19. Multi-item damage validation
315	
316	- Policy has 1 sword. `damages` contains 2 sword entries → error, non-zero exit
317	- Policy has only a sword. `damages` references an amulet → error
318	- Damage amount negative → error
319	
320	### E20. Unknown item type in quote
321	
322	- Quote with `{type: "broomstick"}` → error, non-zero exit, stderr message; no `results`
323	
324	### E21. Rounding payouts down
325	
326	- A payout calculation yielding 350.5 → 350 G
327	
328	### E22. Dragon-material sword ench exactly 8 (from "modifier thresholds")
329	
330	- Dragon material sword, ench 8, damage 1000 → 50% rule applies (ench ≥ 8 takes
331	  precedence over dragon-full), then deductible: 500 − 100 = 400 G
332	
333	---
334	
335	## Questions / Clarifications
336	
337	| ❓ | Resolution |
338	|---|------------|
339	| "Alike" components — same type or family? | Same type only (R2, E4) |
340	| Item-specific modifier scope (curse on whole policy?) | Per-item only (R3, E5) |
341	| First insurance — first ever or first per item? | Per-item, always applies (R3a, E8) |
342	| Multiple items of same type | Allowed; each `damages` entry = separate event (R8, E18, E19) |
343	| Modifier thresholds (exactly 2 yrs loyalty, exactly ench 5 high-ench, exactly ench 8 dragon) | Inclusive at the threshold (≥) |
344	
345	---
346	
347	## Per-test rationale
348	
349	Tests live in `src/claim-office.spec.ts`. The CLI is tested via a child-process
350	invocation of `src/cli.ts`. The test list, ordered simple → complex:
351	
352	1. **Quote: empty items → 5 G (fee only)** — E2 / R10
353	2. **Quote: single plain sword (year=0) → 115 G** — E1, R1, R3a (first-ins +10%)
354	3. **Quote: single plain amulet → 71 G** — E1
355	4. **Quote: single plain staff → 93 G** — E1
356	5. **Quote: single plain potion → 49 G** — E1
357	6. **Quote: 2 runes → 60 G** — E3, R1 (components, no block)
358	7. **Quote: 3 runes block → 71 G** — E3, R1 (block discount)
359	8. **Quote: 4 runes → 115 G** — E3 (no block since not exactly 3)
360	9. **Quote: 7 runes → 198 G (premium rounded up from 197.5)** — E3, E9, R4
361	10. **Quote: 2 runes + 1 moonstone → 88 G (alike = same type only)** — E4, R2
362	11. **Quote: 3 runes + 3 moonstones → 137 G (two separate blocks)** — E4, R2
363	12. **Quote: cursed sword (ench 3, year=0, first contract) → 165 G** — E10, R3 (curse +50%)
364	13. **Quote: cursed sword + plain amulet (year=0) → 231 G (curse on sword only)** — E5, R3 (item-specific curse)
365	14. **Quote: sword ench 5 → 145 G (high-ench applies at exactly 5)** — E6
366	15. **Quote: sword ench 4 → 115 G (no high-ench)** — E6
367	16. **Quote: cursed sword ench 5 → 195 G (both surcharges)** — E6
368	17. **Quote: 2-year customer, plain sword → 95 G (loyalty applies at exactly 2)** — E7, R3 (loyalty)
369	18. **Quote: 3-year customer's second quote, cursed sword ench 7 → 160 G (integration)** — E8, R3 (follow-up + loyalty)
370	19. **Claim: steel sword ench 3, damage 500 → payout 400, remainingCap 1600** — E12, R5
371	20. **Claim: rune damage 200 → payout 100, remainingCap 400** — E12 (components, no special clause)
372	21. **Claim: two damages in one incident (sword 500 + amulet 300) → payout 600** — E11, R5 (deductible per event)
373	22. **Claim: steel sword ench 9, damage 1000 → payout 400 (50% rule)** — E13, R6
374	23. **Claim: dragon sword ench 5, damage 800 → payout 700 (full reimbursement)** — E14, R6
375	24. **Claim: dragon sword ench 9, damage 1000 → payout 400 (50% wins over dragon)** — E15, R6
376	25. **Claim: dragon sword ench 8, damage 1000 → payout 400 (50% applies at exactly 8)** — E22, R6
377	26. **Claim: cap exhaustion — sword, two claims of 1500 → first payout 1400 remCap 600, second payout 600 remCap 0** — E16, R7
378	27. **Claim: cap based on raw insurance values (cursed sword cap = 2000)** — E17, R5
379	28. **Claim: cap unaffected by block discount (sword + 3 runes cap = 3500)** — E17, R5
380	29. **Claim: two swords in policy, both damaged → payout per damage with own deductible** — E18, R8
381	30. **Claim: payout rounding down — calculation yielding 350.5 → payout 350** — E21, R4
382	31. **CLI: quote scenario via stdin/stdout produces `{results:[{premium:...}]}`** — R9
383	32. **CLI: claim scenario via stdin/stdout produces `{results:[..., {payout, remainingCap}]}`** — R9
384	33. **CLI: unknown item type (`broomstick`) → non-zero exit, stderr message, no results on stdout** — R9, E20
385	34. **CLI: claim references item type not in policy → non-zero exit, stderr** — R9, E19
386	35. **CLI: claim has negative damage amount → non-zero exit, stderr** — R9, E19
387	36. **CLI: claim has more damages of a type than policy contains → non-zero exit, stderr** — R8, E19
388	
389	The CLI tests (31–36) exercise the binding interface contract from the spec.
390	For test 30, we construct a scenario where the payout math yields a fractional
391	value (e.g. a 50%-reimbursement scenario where damage is odd).
392

1	# TDD Journal
2	1 R | empty item list returns premium 5 G | comp:OK | run:OK | -
3	1 G | return 5 hardcoded | passing:1
4	2 R | plain sword 115 G | comp:OK (no error, fn already exists) | run:OK | -
5	2 G | return 115 hardcoded when items non-empty | passing:2
6	3 R | plain amulet 71 G | comp:OK (no error, fn exists) | run:OK | -
7	3 G | return 71 when first item is amulet | passing:3
8	4 R | plain staff 93 G | comp:OK (no error, fn exists) | run:OK | -
9	4 G | return 93 when first item is staff | passing:4
10	5 R | plain potion 49 G | comp:OK (no error, fn exists) | run:OK | -
11	5 G | generalize: BASE_PREMIUMS lookup + 10% first-ins per item + fee | passing:5
12	6 R | 2 runes 60 G | comp:OK (no error, fn exists) | run:OK (NaN vs 60) | -
13	6 G | add rune:25 to BASE_PREMIUMS | passing:6
14	7 R | 3 runes block 71 G | comp:OK (no error, fn exists) | run:OK (87.5 vs 71) | -
15	7 G | when runeCount==3 apply block adjustment (60-75=-15) to baseSum | passing:7
16	8 R | 4 runes 115 G (no block) | comp:OK (fn exists) | run:OK (test passes immediately, runeCount!=3 bypasses block, 100+10+5=115) | -
17	9 R | 7 runes 198 G (rounding up from 197.5) | comp:OK (fn exists) | run:OK (197.5 vs 198) | -
18	9 G | wrap final return in Math.ceil | passing:9
19	10 R | 2 runes + 1 moonstone 88 G | comp:OK (fn exists) | run:OK (NaN vs 88) | -
20	10 G | add moonstone:25 to BASE_PREMIUMS | passing:10
21	11 R | 3 runes + 3 moonstones 137 G (two separate blocks) | comp:OK (fn exists) | run:OK (154 vs 137; moonstone block not applied) | -
22	11 G | generalize block logic: iterate component types, apply discount per type with exactly 3 | passing:11
